bounded_excerpt: The evidence- based recommendations outlined below outlines the standard follow -up procedures for uveal melanoma, class 1 (low risk) surveillance, and are intended to assist you in providing optimal cancer follow -up care for your patient; these recommendations are not intended to be a substitute for clinical judgment.
quoted_span: The evidence- based recommendations outlined below outlines the standard follow -up procedures for uveal melanoma, class 1 (low risk) surveillance, and are intended to assist you in providing optimal cancer follow -up care for your patient; these recommendations are not intended to be a substitute for clinical judgment.
quoted_text: The evidence- based recommendations outlined below outlines the standard follow -up procedures for uveal melanoma, class 1 (low risk) surveillance, and are intended to assist you in providing optimal cancer follow -up care for your patient; these recommendations are not intended to be a substitute for clinical judgment.

bounded_excerpt: Surveillance for Cancer Recurrence Please continue systemic monitoring of this patient. As part of the minimum recommended follow -up, your patient requires indefinitely :
quoted_span: Surveillance for Cancer Recurrence Please continue systemic monitoring of this patient. As part of the minimum recommended follow -up, your patient requires indefinitely :
quoted_text: Surveillance for Cancer Recurrence Please continue systemic monitoring of this patient. As part of the minimum recommended follow -up, your patient requires indefinitely :

bounded_excerpt: • Annual full physical (including skin examination) • Annual CBC/liver function tests, chest x -ray and ultrasound of the abdomen
quoted_span: • Annual full physical (including skin examination) • Annual CBC/liver function tests, chest x -ray and ultrasound of the abdomen
quoted_text: • Annual full physical (including skin examination) • Annual CBC/liver function tests, chest x -ray and ultrasound of the abdomen

bounded_excerpt: Patients presenting with any symptoms or signs of recurrence should be investigated and referred back to the treating oncologist.
quoted_span: Patients presenting with any symptoms or signs of recurrence should be investigated and referred back to the treating oncologist.
quoted_text: Patients presenting with any symptoms or signs of recurrence should be investigated and referred back to the treating oncologist.

bounded_excerpt: Last revis ion: Nov 2024 Uveal Melanoma Class 1 Transfer of Care Letter – Physician AHS ONC TRANSFER OF CARE- UVEAL -MELANOMA -CLASS -1-PHYSICIAN 3 Guideline Resource Unit Complications and Late Effects of Cancer Treatment The table below outlines some other common general complications of cancer treatment: Complication Actions Fatigue • Fatigue should start to improve within months of treatment completion. • Persistent or recurrent fatigue warrants further work -up to rule out other potential causes. • For more information please refer to the Cancer -Related Fatigue Guideline . • Consider referral to Alberta Cancer Exercise Program ( www.albertacancerexercise.com ) Psychosocial distress • Increasing helplessness /hopelessness, distress, anxiety or depression may be present. • Patients experiencing these symptoms should be encouraged to inform their oncology treatment team for appropriate psychosocial referral.

bounded_excerpt: Healthy Lifestyle Recommendations : Your patient is encouraged to lead a healthy lifestyle. Visit Cancer Care Alberta’s supportive care and living well website or the After Treatment book for more information. Here are some evidence informed recommendations about modifiable lifestyle factors for your information:
quoted_span: Healthy Lifestyle Recommendations : Your patient is encouraged to lead a healthy lifestyle. Visit Cancer Care Alberta’s supportive care and living well website or the After Treatment book for more information. Here are some evidence informed recommendations about modifiable lifestyle factors for your information:
quoted_text: Healthy Lifestyle Recommendations : Your patient is encouraged to lead a healthy lifestyle. Visit Cancer Care Alberta’s supportive care and living well website or the After Treatment book for more information. Here are some evidence informed recommendations about modifiable lifestyle factors for your information:

bounded_excerpt: Modifiable Lifestyle Factor Recommendations Body Weight • Body mass index (BMI): 18.5 -25 kg/m2 • Waist circumference: less than 80 cm for women / less than 94 cm for men. Physical Activity • Try to be active for 2.5 hours (150 minutes) every week. • Spread out exercise throughout the day and week, such as 30 minutes 5 days a week. • Focus on moderate (brisk walking) to vigorous activity (jogging).
quoted_span: Modifiable Lifestyle Factor Recommendations Body Weight • Body mass index (BMI): 18.5 -25 kg/m2 • Waist circumference: less than 80 cm for women / less than 94 cm for men. Physical Activity • Try to be active for 2.5 hours (150 minutes) every week. • Spread out exercise throughout the day and week, such as 30 minutes 5 days a week. • Focus on moderate (brisk walking) to vigorous activity (jogging).
quoted_text: Modifiable Lifestyle Factor Recommendations Body Weight • Body mass index (BMI): 18.5 -25 kg/m2 • Waist circumference: less than 80 cm for women / less than 94 cm for men. Physical Activity • Try to be active for 2.5 hours (150 minutes) every week. • Spread out exercise throughout the day and week, such as 30 minutes 5 days a week. • Focus on moderate (brisk walking) to vigorous activity (jogging).

bounded_excerpt: Last revis ion: Nov 2024 Uveal Melanoma Class 1 Transfer of Care Letter – Physician AHS ONC TRANSFER OF CARE- UVEAL -MELANOMA -CLASS -1-PHYSICIAN 4 Guideline Resource Unit Nutrition • Avoid sugary drinks and foods. • Eat a variety of vegetables, fruits, whole grains, and legumes. • Limit consumption of red meats (such as beef, pork, and lamb), and avoid processed meats. • Limit consumption of salty foods and foods processed with salt. Dietary Supplements/ Bone Health • Vitamin D: 1000 - 2000 IU per day. • Calcium: 1000 mg per day (from all sources). • Treatment and follow up as per Canadian Osteoporosis Guidelines. Alcohol Limit alcohol consumption (<1 drink/day, <3 drinks/week). Smoking Practice smoking cessation. For help contact Alberta Quits 1 -877-710-QUIT (7848) or visit www.albertaquits.ca and www.ahs.ca/guru for the clinical practice guideline. Sun Exposure • Advise on avoidance of excessive or potentially harmful UV exposure. • Advocate for the use of sunscreen and sunglasses. • Advise against the use of indoor tanning beds. • Check skin regularly for suspicious lesion. Immunizations • Annual non -live influenza vaccination unless contraindicated. • Other vaccinations as a
